THE MATTERS ARE CALLED. ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE TENTATIVE RULINGS ARE ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (EFFECTIVELY A DEMURRER) IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 4, 2021. AT THAT TIME, THE MOTION WILL BE MOOT IF PLAINTIFF FILES THE AMENDED COMPLAINT BEING AUTHORIZED TODAY. DEFENDANTS SAY THEY WILL DEMUR TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT, WHICH IS A PROPER APPROACH. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER BORER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUMENT IS HELD, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER BORER'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA COURTS "ARE BOUND TO APPLY A POLICY OF GREAT LIBERALITY IN PERMITTING AMENDMENTS TO THE COMPLAINT AT ANY STAGE OF THE PROCEEDING, UP TO AND INCLUDING TRIAL." (ATKINSON V. ELK CORP. (2003) 109 CAL.APP.4TH 739, 761.) THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE IS FIVE MONTHS OUT AND THE ADDED DISCOVERY DEFENDANTS SAY THEY "MAY" TAKE APPEARS MINIMAL. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Jun-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS RASIER LLC) and RASIER-CA, LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS RASIER-CA LLC)'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS:

Off-calendar. First amended complaint filed May 27, 2021.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Plaintiff Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint With Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar May-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Annie Wu, Esq. of the Veen Firm, 415.673.4800, for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Steven R. Disharoon, Esq. of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP, 707.559.6750, for the Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Raiser;
Atty. Daphne Hall, Esq. of Clinton & Clinton, 562.216.5000, for the Defendant Chung Him Leung.

Plaintiff Christopher Borer's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

The matters are called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff Christopher Borer's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. California courts "are bound to apply a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint at any stage of the proceeding, up to and including trial." (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) The current trial date is five months out and the added discovery defendants say they "may" take appears minimal.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar May-07-2021 Continued To Jun-04-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Annie Wu, Esq. of the Veen Firm, 415.673.4800, for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Steven R. Disharoon, Esq. of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP, 707.559.6750, for the Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Raiser;
Atty. Daphne Hall, Esq. of Clinton & Clinton, 562.216.5000, for the Defendant Chung Him Leung.

DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS RASIER LLC), RASIER-CA, LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS RASIER-CA LLC)'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

The matters are called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings (effectively a demurrer) is continued to June 4, 2021. At that time, the motion will be moot if plaintiff files the amended complaint being authorized today. Defendants say they will demur to the amended complaint, which is a proper approach.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
